title: Ambush at Mos Eisley

{{Quote|Good morning, Wedge. The rest of Rogue Squadron is still back at base, but I thought we could take an early morning run through Beggar's Canyon.|Luke Skywalker, in a transmission to Wedge Antilles|<ref name="RS" />}}
The '''Ambush at Mos Eisley''' was conducted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] over the [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]]. [[0 ABY/Legends|Six months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]—an Imperial battle station—members of the elite Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] group [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] were taking an off-duty morning run through the infamous [[Beggar's Canyon/Legends|Beggar's Canyon]] outside of Mos Eisley. The Empire deployed detachments of [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droids]] and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]] to Tatooine to bomb the spaceport and the surrounding areas to root out [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a member of Rogue Squadron and the Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] responsible for the Death Star's destruction. The [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] of the fighter squad provided support for the homesteads and native [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] under Imperial fire. The team then made a run through Mos Eisley, shooting down the six TIE bombers before the city could be destroyed.

==Prelude==
Ever since [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] became a [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|thorn in its side]] by destroying the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] kept an eye on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Acting on a tip from an unsavory [[Unidentified bounty hunter (Imperial tip)|bounty hunter]], the Empire dispatched [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] and [[Bomber/Legends|bombers]] to root out Skywalker's [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] by terrorizing civilians and searching for clues to Skywalker's whereabouts.<ref name="Official Guide" />

==The battle==
===Cleanup of Imperial patrols===
{{Quote|Mos Eisley is under attack; we need to assist!|Luke Skywalker, to his wingmates|<ref name="RS" />}}
[[File:Rogue_Squadron_Jabba_Palace.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Rogue Squadron patrolling the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]].]]
[[0 ABY/Legends|Six months after]] the Battle of Yavin, the newly formed Alliance to Restore the Republic [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] unit [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] visited [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Tatooine. [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Luke Skywalker, the [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]], initially planned to simply patrol the outskirts of the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. As three squadron members flew in an early morning run through the area, [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], one of the Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], spotted several [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droids]] opening fire on a number of [[Moisture farm/Legends|homesteads]]. The pilots were caught off guard, and one member of the squadron was shot down near [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]], headquarters of the [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|gangster]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], but the pilot survived the crash-landing. Rogue Squadron fanned out and destroyed most of the eighteen probe droids in the area. The squadron flew by two civilian [[T-16 skyhopper/Legends|T-16 skyhoppers]] in [[Beggar's Canyon/Legends|Beggar's Canyon]].<ref name="RS" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)}}
As Skywalker neared the last of the homesteads, he veered off to the area around the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]],<ref name="RS" /> site of the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]]—a giant, flesh-eating beast<ref name="SWE6">{{Film|VI}}</ref>—and knocked out the 4-member [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] and [[Dewback trooper/Legends|dewback-riding stormtrooper]] patrol there. The Rebel pilot then destroyed the last three probe droids, saving a nearby [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]] transport.<ref name="RS" />
{{Endgame}}

===Bombing of Mos Eisley===
[[File:RS_Mos_eisley_2.jpg|thumb|right|220px|TIE bombers attacking Mos Eisley.]]
After the probe droids were taken out, a six-fighter squadron of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]] began an attack on Mos Eisley, flying in a circular pattern to drop [[Proton bomb/Legends|proton bombs]] on vital parts of the city. Skywalker ordered Rogue Squadron to assist, and they quickly flew overhead; the bombers were no match for Rogue Squadron's [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]], and all the bombers were shot from the sky.<ref name="RS" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|We did it! We saved Mos Eisley! Nice work, Rogue Squadron. We might make a name for ourselves after all…|Luke Skywalker, at the end of the mission|<ref name="RS" />}}
After the ambush force was destroyed, Skywalker thought that the newly formed Rogue Squadron would go on to make a name for itself. With the success of their mission, the Rogues were [[Rendezvous on Barkhesh|assigned]] to escort supplies on the planet [[Barkhesh/Legends|Barkhesh]] shortly afterward.<ref name="RS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Ambush at Mos Eisley was the first mission in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']], which was released in [[1998]]. Due to the game mechanics during the mission, it is possible to destroy Jabba's Palace and other civilian targets, although this is [[Canon|non-canon]]; if enough civilian targets are destroyed by the player's actions or lack thereof, the mission will end in failure. To achieve the gold medal on this mission, it is necessary to veer over to the side by the Great Pit of Carkoon and take out extra stormtroopers, although such an action is optional. The X-wing is the default craft for the mission.<ref name="RS" />

The game's [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide|official guide]] suggests the players prioritize attacking the probe droids which target the civilian structures to keep a high "friendlies saved" score. After the droids are destroyed, the guide tells the player to activate the speed boost by closing the [[S-foils/Legends|S-foils]] on the X-wing. Once the player reaches Mos Eisley, the guide suggests targeting the second group of bombers first, as it is closer and the first group targets the player's [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmen]].<ref name="Official Guide" />
